requirement.original: Can the feedbackcoach be on top then normal company below. Such as: FeedbackCoach / NormalCompany - Marketing Team
requirement.clarified: Stack the two inner row wrappers in DashboardHeader vertically (each on its own block-level line, centered) so the Sparkles+FeedbackCoach title row appears above the NC+company row. Behavior must hold on both desktop 1440x900 and mobile 390x844.
requirement.what: Change two Tailwind classes (inline-flex -> flex) on the two inner row wrappers in DashboardHeader so both rows occupy their own line.
requirement.why: User explicitly requested vertical stacking. Cycle 1 left the two rows as inline-flex which causes desktop side-by-side collapse.

excluded: any structural HTML change in DashboardHeader
removal or modification of text-center on header
the NC circle's own classes (h-8 w-8 bg-blue-600 ...)

success_criteria: DashboardHeader's title row and NC row each render on their own visual line at desktop 1440x900
Both rows render on their own line at mobile 390x844 (already true; must remain true)
Both rows are horizontally centered within the header
All Cycle 1 deliverables (H1 text, font-size, NC circle, Briefcase icon, tenure strings) are unchanged
constraints: Tailwind utility classes only — no new CSS file
No new wrappers, no new spans, no new IDs
Diff must be exactly two single-word substitutions

root_cause_analysis.symptom: On desktop the title row and NC row collapse onto a single horizontal visual line instead of stacking
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Both inner row wrappers use `inline-flex` (inline-level box). When parent <header> has enough horizontal space, two inline-level boxes lay out side-by-side. To force a stack, the boxes must be block-level (`flex`).
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: c17716f — Cycle 1 commit that introduced inline-flex on both wrappers (per spec-20260426-102329 Section 3)
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Cycle 1's BA chose inline-flex to keep each row's content as a centered group. The original spec did not require row-stacking, so QA accepted the layout.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: User now wants stacked vertical layout; inline-flex prevents the rows from claiming their own line on viewports with sufficient horizontal width.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Introduced 2026-04-26 (Cycle 1 commit c17716f). Reported 2026-04-26 by user post-commit.
observations: title-row wrapper className contains 'inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2' (observed: Read app/page.tsx:10)
NC-row wrapper className contains 'mt-2 inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 text-sm text-slate-500' (observed: Read app/page.tsx:14)
QA Cycle 1 Section 4 reports the desktop side-by-side collapse (observed: spec-20260426-102329.md Section 4 'Layout note' line)
Mobile viewport 390x844 already stacks because narrow width forces the second inline-flex box to wrap (observed: QA Cycle 1 screenshot dashboard-mobile-390x844.png)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: User explicitly references the dashboard header strings 'FeedbackCoach' and 'NormalCompany - Marketing Team'. These strings exist verbatim in app/page.tsx:12 (h1) and L21 (span inside NC row). The DashboardHeader function spans L7-28 of app/page.tsx. QA Cycle 1 already measured and confirmed the rendered output for these elements.
root_cause_analysis.located_source: app/page.tsx:7-28 (DashboardHeader function); specifically the two inner row wrappers at L10 and L14

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: N/A — not a regression. The 'symptom' was introduced as part of the legitimate Cycle 1 implementation that satisfied the (then-existing) acceptance criteria. User added a NEW criterion in this cycle.

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Can the feedbackcoach be on top
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Title row (Sparkles + FeedbackCoach) renders on its own block-level line, horizontally centered, on both desktop 1440x900 and mobile 390x844.
NC circle + 'NormalCompany — Marketing Team' span renders on its own block-level line BELOW the title row, horizontally centered, on both desktop 1440x900 and mobile 390x844.

component_chain.role: DashboardHeader functional component — owns the entire two-row + footer-paragraph header markup
component_chain.evidence: Read app/page.tsx returned the full DashboardHeader source; lines 10 and 14 hold the two inline-flex divs

component_chain.role: Home (default export) — renders <DashboardHeader /> and the cards section

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Decorative icon hidden from AT — must remain on the title row
AT label for the NC circle (visual text 'NC' is a glyph, not a word)

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Logged in as Alex, Marketing Team Manager
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Footer paragraph; must remain a sibling of the two row divs, not nested inside either

evidence.observed: User: 'Can the feedbackcoach be on top then normal company below. Such as: FeedbackCoach / NormalCompany - Marketing Team'

measured.value: title-row wrapper uses 'inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2'; NC-row wrapper uses 'mt-2 inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 text-sm text-slate-500'

expected.value: Both wrappers use block-level 'flex' (drop the 'inline-' prefix on both)
expected.source: User's verbatim mockup 'FeedbackCoach / NormalCompany - Marketing Team' (slash = line break) in the orchestrator prompt for dev-20260426-123337

evidence.gap: Two single-word class substitutions: 'inline-flex' -> 'flex' on app/page.tsx:10 AND app/page.tsx:14

reference_source.description: User's verbatim mockup describing the desired layout (slash-separated lines)
reference_source.url_or_location: Orchestrator prompt body for dev-20260426-123337 (cycle 2 of spec-20260426-102329)

attempts.proposed_solution: Implement CHANGES 1+2 with inline-flex wrappers, satisfying the spec's literal CHANGE 2 acceptance (NC left of company text, gap-2, vertically centered).
attempts.actual_change: app/page.tsx:10 and L14 use inline-flex; produced single-line desktop layout.
attempts.outcome: PASSED Cycle 1 QA. Not a failure — the acceptance criteria did not mandate row stacking. User then added a NEW requirement.

novelty_check.rationale: Same layer (L1 cosmetic Tailwind), but the action is different: Cycle 1 chose inline-flex (correct then). Cycle 2 changes to flex to satisfy a NEW user criterion. Contract D's 'must use a different layer' rule applies to retries of FAILED attempts; Cycle 1 was a PASS, so layer-level reuse is correct here. The change is qualitatively novel because it answers a new question, not the same question.

context.codebase_state: Working tree clean post-c17716f. Only INDEX.md modified (auto-sync hook).

development_approach.strategy: Two single-word Tailwind class substitutions in app/page.tsx. No structural change. No other file touched.
development_approach.validation_approach: Playwright at desktop 1440x900 and mobile 390x844 reads getComputedStyle(div).display for both inner rows (must be 'flex'), reads bounding boxes (NC row top > title row bottom; both centered within header), and captures screenshots.

research_findings.reason: Trivial CSS display-mode flip with unambiguous user mockup; no design discretion or pattern research needed.
